channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ukraine-US talks on ending war with Russia start in Saudi Arabia as Kyiv launches huge drone attack
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Senior officials from Ukraine and the United States opened talks Tuesday on how to end Moscow’s three-year war against Kyiv, hours after Russian air defenses shot down more than 300 Ukrainian drones in the biggest such attack since the Kremlin ordered the full-scale invas
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli fire kills 4 Palestinians in Gaza Strip, 3 in the occupied West Bank
item.description: Israeli fire has killed four people and wounded 14 in the Gaza Strip over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas has largely held.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Republicans are marching ahead with a government funding bill despite Democratic opposition
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans will face a critical test of their unity when a spending bill that would avoid a partial government shutdown and keep federal agencies funded through September comes up for a vote. Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump considers easing safety screenings for chemicals
item.description: The Trump administration will consider easing regulations under which it considers the safety of existing chemicals, drawing concerns from public health advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KREX 5

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Father of missing US student Sudiksha Konanki asks for wider investigation in Dominican Republic
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The father of a 20-year-old University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki, who went missing in the popular Dominican Republic tourist town of Punta Cana while on spring break with friends, said he has asked authorities to widen their investigation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Garlin Gilchrist announces Democratic bid to become Michigan's first Black governor
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Garlin Gilchrist II, a Democrat from Detroit who is Michigan's first Black lieutenant governor, announced he's jumping into the crowded 2026 race for governor on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medical helicopter crash in Mississippi kills 3
item.description: MADISON COUNTY, Miss. (AP) — A medical transport helicopter crashed in Mississippi on Monday, killing a pilot and two hospital workers on board, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KTLA 5

item.title: 3 killed after medical helicopter crashes in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died after a medical helicopter crashed near the Natchez Trace in Madison County, Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abbey Gate terror suspect remains in custody, faces charges connected to deaths of 13 US service members
item.description: Suspected Abbey Gate terror plotter Mohammad Sharifullah appeared in federal court on Monday, where the judge ordered he be held pending further hearings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Statements man charged in Atlanta spa killings made post-arrest can be used at trial, judge rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge on Monday rejected an attempt by attorneys for a man accused of killing four women at Atlanta massage businesses to keep a jury from hearing statements he made after his arrest.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 killed after medical helicopter crashes near Natchez Trace
item.description: MADISON COUNTY, Miss. (WJTV) - Three people have died after a medical helicopter crashed near the Natchez Trace in Madison County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: An office known for enforcing special education is now focused on Trump's political priorities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department's Office for Civil Rights is known best for enforcing the right to disability services across America’s schools. But under President Donald Trump, it’s taking a frontline role in his political battles.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida house collapses with 2 people inside during tornado
item.description: The storm knocked out power and took down trees in the area, as well as dealing minor damage to some nearby structures.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: "Gross abuses of executive power raise serious concerns over the freedoms of peaceful assembly, expression and association," the group wrote of the U.S. rating.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: Harvard freezes hiring amid financial 'uncertainties' driven by Trump policies
item.description: Harvard University says it is freezing hiring for faculty and staff amid financial “uncertainties” driven by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Juarez police officer injured in body shop attack that left 3 dead
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Three men were killed and two injured by gunfire during a shootout Sunday at a mechanical shop in Juarez, Mexico. Juarez police said at least two men arrived in a vehicle at the business in the Heroes de la Revolucion neighborhood around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump DOT chief rejects inclusion of environmental justice factors in infrastructure decisions
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy on Monday rescinded Biden-era memos that he said called on state agencies to factor a “social justice and environmental agenda” into infrastructure project decisions.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Greenland votes amid Trump invitation to join US: What to know
item.description: Greenland's upcoming national election could set the stage for a later referendum on its future control, as President Trump has renewed his quest to take over the natural resources-rich arctic land.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: Cuomo & Carlson: The Conversation continues
item.description: Despite Carlson and Cuomo being critical of each other in the past, the two sit down to discuss issues like Russia-Ukraine war, declassified JFK and Epstein files and more.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri on the verge of taking over St. Louis police
item.description: The move comes nearly 13 years after voters returned police oversight to local leaders for the first time since the Civil War.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: X hit by 'massive cyberattack,' Musk says amid intermittent outages Monday
item.description: Elon Musk says there was a "massive cyberattack against X" on Monday amid several outages to the social media site.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Fire risk remains high on Long Island after brush fires
item.description: The fire danger remains high on Long Island as officials work to quell wind-driven brush fires that broke out Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KRON4

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 days of clashes and revenge killings in Syria leave more than 1,000 people dead
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The death toll from two days of clashes between Syrian security forces and loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad and revenge killings that followed has risen to more than 1,000, a war monitoring group said Saturday, making it one of the deadliest acts of violence since Syria’s con
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: President Trump suggests he may not change daylight saving time
item.description: President Donald Trump indicated Thursday he may not push to end daylight saving time as Americans get ready to spring forward this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9
